title: Expression sandbox escape leading to RCE via spread of Node globals
description: n8n evaluates user-authored workflow expressions through the Tournament engine, with PrototypeSanitizer (an AST after-hook) and a substituted EMPTY_CONTEXT meant to deny access to dangerous Node.js globals such as process. The pre-patch sanitizer only neutralized process (not require/module/Buffer) and only recognized reserved identifiers in plain Identifier positions; it had no visitSpreadElement/visitSpreadProperty handling. As a result, an authenticated user could spread a global, e.g. ({...process}) or via destructuring/assignment/for-in bindings, to recover the real Node.js process object and pivot to arbitrary command execution on the n8n host. This extends the earlier CVE-2025-68613 (GHSA-v98v-ff95-f3cp) sandbox-escape class.
